creator: johan@stromnet.se
text:
Hi, seen the same issue.

Also reported here: 

https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1364730

Which also references the same patch, from amavisd-new mailing list:
https://lists.amavis.org/pipermail/amavis-users/2016-July/004428.html


From the lack of patch acceptance or even response in the upstream project, I'll probably look into leaving amavisd for simpler setup (not really using all features of it)...

Maybe I'm blind but... is there no git/svn repo? A bugtracker? Or...source files.. is the source really handled in a file with 32k lines of code? Hopefully not!
